mnemonics.title: Constructor Overloading Rule
mnemonics.content: Remember: Same name, DIFFERENT signature. The compiler uses parameter list to decide which constructor to call.
When copying objects: PASS BY REFERENCE to avoid infinite loop. Use CONST to preserve original. Think: 'CRaP' - Const Reference as Parameter.
mnemonics.title: Copy Constructor Signature

mnemonics.content: Deep vs Shallow: Shallow = Share the SAME address. Deep = Duplicate the DATA to NEW address. Remember: Shallow shares 'shadow', Deep creates distinct 'depth'.
mnemonics.title: Deep vs Shallow Copy Memory Aid

mnemonics.content: Initialization List Order: Members initialize in DECLARATION order in class, NOT in initialization list order. Remember: List order doesn't matter, declaration order does.
mnemonics.title: Initialization List Execution Order

acronyms.expansion: Copy Constructor Takes const reference parameter
Deep Independent Pointer - deep copy creates independent data
acronyms.usage: Used when class contains dynamic memory allocation

visualTips.visualization: Think of multiple doors (constructors) to enter a building (class). Each door takes different keys (parameters). Compiler gives you the right door based on your key.
Imagine photocopying a document. The copy is a new document with same content but different paper (memory).
A relay race where one runner (constructor) passes the baton (execution) to another runner (another constructor) to finish the race.
Think of filling a form before entering - data members are pre-filled before constructor body runs. Essential for const and reference 'read-only' members.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant with multiple套餐 (combo meals). You can order different combinations - each combo is a different constructor serving the same 'restaurant' (class).
Like cloning a plant. The new plant (copied object) looks identical but has its own root system (independent memory). Shallow copy would be two plants sharing the same pot.
Like a default phone ringtone - comes preset without you configuring anything. Used when you don't specify preferences.
Like a VIP-only entrance to an event. Only people inside (class methods) can create objects, outsiders cannot.
